cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs LOG0 dynamic-offset log benchmark onto an offset-independent receipt-log witness with 0 topic(s), 0 payload bytes, and exact payload proof.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark LOG0 with offset=GAS % 7, no topics, and log_size=0.

RPC mapping: deploy a small runtime that computes GAS % 7 immediately before LOG, then emits the zero-length payload. The dynamic offset is still present in bytecode but cannot affect an empty log payload.
Witness keeps full `data` equality because the payload is small enough for stable diffs.

Admitted because the dynamic offset is present in the runtime and the emitted payload is independent of its value; non-zero dynamic-offset payloads remain blocked until gas-derived byte-window observation is mapped.
For LOG0 the upstream zeros_topic branch does not affect the receipt because the opcode consumes no topics; both branch labels are still tracked as distinct upstream cases.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs LOG0 fixed-offset log benchmark onto a receipt-log witness with 0 topic(s), 0 payload bytes, and exact payload proof.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark LOG0 with a fixed memory offset, no topics, and log_size=0.
RPC mapping: deploy a small runtime that emits exactly one receipt log at offset 0 from zero-initialized memory.
Witness keeps full `data` equality because the payload is small enough for stable diffs.
Admitted because fixed offset removes the gas-sensitive dynamic-addressing branch and the remaining topic/payload semantics are fully observable in the receipt.
For LOG0 the upstream zeros_topic branch does not affect the receipt because the opcode consumes no topics; both branch labels are still tracked as distinct upstream cases.

RPC mapping: deploy a small runtime that emits exactly one receipt log at offset 0. The runtime seeds the first 1048576 memory byte(s) with 0xff before logging.
Witness records `data_digest` plus `data_length_bytes` so large payload variants stay truthful without noisy full-byte diffs.
Admitted because fixed offset removes the gas-sensitive dynamic-addressing branch and the remaining topic/payload semantics are fully observable in the receipt.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs LOG4 fixed-offset log benchmark onto a receipt-log witness with 4 topic(s), 1048576 payload bytes, and digest payload proof.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark LOG4 with a fixed memory offset, 4 repeated zero topic word(s), and log_size=1048576.
RPC mapping: deploy a small runtime that emits exactly one receipt log at offset 0. The runtime seeds the first 1048576 memory byte(s) with 0xff before logging.
Witness records `data_digest` plus `data_length_bytes` so large payload variants stay truthful without noisy full-byte diffs.
Admitted because fixed offset removes the gas-sensitive dynamic-addressing branch and the remaining topic/payload semantics are fully observable in the receipt.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs LOG4 dynamic-offset log benchmark onto an offset-independent receipt-log witness with 4 topic(s), 1048576 payload bytes, and digest payload proof.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark LOG4 with offset=GAS % 7, 4 repeated non-zero topic word(s), and log_size=1048576.
RPC mapping: deploy a small runtime that computes GAS % 7 immediately before LOG without pre-seeding memory, so every possible dynamic offset reads the same all-zero payload.
Witness records `data_digest` plus `data_length_bytes` so large payload variants stay truthful without noisy full-byte diffs.

Admitted because the dynamic offset is present in the runtime and the emitted payload is independent of its value; non-zero dynamic-offset payloads remain blocked until gas-derived byte-window observation is mapped.
